foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Portal vein formation, hepatic sinusoidal architecture, and portosystemic anastomosis sites are fundamental to understanding pre-hepatic, hepatic, and post-hepatic causes

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Cirrhosis causing portal hypertension frequently coexists with cholestatic jaundice; shared assessment with LFTs, imaging, and Child-Pugh scoring

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: HCC arises in cirrhotic livers with portal hypertension; portal vein thrombosis by tumor worsens portal pressures and contraindicates resection

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Hypersplenism from portal hypertension causes pancytopenia; splenectomy or devascularization procedures may be indicated

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Decompensated portal hypertension (recurrent variceal bleeding, refractory ascites) is a primary indication for liver transplantation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Portal pyemia from intra-abdominal infections can cause both portal vein thrombosis and liver abscess, representing pre-hepatic portal hypertension

objectives: IM5.6: Describe and discuss the pathophysiology, clinical evolution and
SU28.10: Describe the applied anatomy of liver. Describe the Clinical features,
SU28.11: Describe the applied anatomy of Spleen. Describe the clinical features,
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Portal Hypertension across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
